steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank fills "respective ______ former in a repository..." — the word "species" ends the main clause, and "the former in a repository... and the latter in a repository..." is a noun phrase elaborating on where those specimens are preserved. The punctuation after "species" must correctly attach that explanatory phrase.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'species, the': A comma before "the former..." correctly introduces an appositive noun phrase that specifies where each specimen is kept. The phrase has no main verb, so it cannot stand alone — a comma is the right connector. ✓
  • Choice B — 'species, and the': Adding "and" implies a second independent clause is following, but "the former in a repository..." has no verb — it's a noun phrase, not a clause. ✗
  • Choice C — 'species. The': A period creates a sentence fragment: "The former in a repository at Washington, DC's Museum of Natural History and the latter in a repository at London's Natural History Museum" has no verb. ✗
  • Choice D — 'species; the': A semicolon must connect two independent clauses. "The former in a repository..." is a noun phrase with no verb, so a semicolon is grammatically incorrect here. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
